{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_16b03f45683a4c7fb9b0fd68","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ADR-002: Close detection gaps that let the multipart-upload corruption bug run 40 days undetected\n2\t\n3\t**Status:** Accepted\n4\t**Date:** 2026-07-11\n5\t\n6\t## Context\n7\t\n8\tCommits `b96d7eb`, `3a1526a`, and `c66e264` (landed 2026-06-10/11, versions 0.1.35–~0.1.41) fixed a real correctness bug: ARMOR only routed multipart-upload `POST` calls; a standard client's `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` fell through to plain `PutObject`. Each part got stored as if it were the whole object (\"last part wins\") while B2's real multipart upload never completed. Any object ≥5MiB written through an affected ARMOR instance via multipart semantics was silently corrupted — the write reported success, but the stored bytes were wrong.\n9\t\n10\t`ord-devimprint`'s ARMOR deployment was on `0.1.19` (last bumped 2026-05-31, before the fix) and was never caught up. This was discovered 2026-07-10/11 while investigating why `queue-api`'s litestream backup couldn't be restored: the backup's only surviving snapshot didn't decode, and two more objects were corrupted live *during the investigation*. Net effect: **queue-api's B2 backup chain had no valid restore point for a ~40-day window, and this was never flagged by anything running in production.** Full incident trail in project memory (`project_commitgraph_split`).\n11\t\n12\tThis same fix had already been deployed to `iad-native-ads` after an earlier incident there (barman WAL archiving). `ord-devimprint` was simply missed in that remediation pass. Two more clusters (`iad-ci` at 0.1.24, `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager` at 0.1.13) are still on old versions and have not been checked.\n13\t\n14\t### Why existing safeguards didn't catch this\n15\t\n16\tARMOR already has real integrity infrastructure — this incident is not a design gap in the crypto/verification model, it's a **coverage** gap in what that infrastructure actually exercises:\n17\t\n18\t1. **The self-healing canary monitor** (`internal/canary`) has been running continuously in production since Phase 1, checking upload→download→decrypt→HMAC-verify→SHA-verify every 5 minutes, and has been reporting healthy the entire time. It uses a fixed `canarySize` of 1024 bytes (`internal/server/server.go:117`) and calls `backend.Put()` directly — a single-object write. **It structurally never exercises `CreateMultipartUpload`/`UploadPart`/`CompleteMultipartUpload`**, which is exactly where the bug lived. A monitor built to catch exactly this class of regression has a blind spot sized precisely to miss it.\n19\t2. **`TestMultipartUpload`** (`tests/integration/integration_test.go:525`) already does a real 3-part, 15MB multipart upload/download cycle — but only asserts `ContentLength` matches the expected size. It never reads back and compares the actual bytes against what was uploaded. A corruption that happens to preserve object length (or a test/CI setup where this particular test isn't gating deploys) can pass silently.\n20\t3. **No mechanism tracks version drift** across ARMOR's several cluster deployments. A correctness fix landed on `main` and reached one cluster after a prior incident; nothing flagged that three other clusters (`ord-devimprint`, `iad-ci`, `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager`) were still running the vulnerable version.\n21\t\n22\t## Decision\n23\t\n24\tClose all three gaps. This ADR does **not** propose changes to the encryption/HMAC/provenance design — that infrastructure is sound for correctly-routed requests. It closes the coverage and operational gaps that let a routing regression run undetected.\n25\t\n26\t1. **Extend the canary monitor to cover the multipart path.** Add a second, longer-interval check (e.g., once per hour, alongside the existing 5-minute small-object check) that uploads a canary object sized just above the multipart threshold via real `CreateMultipartUpload`/`UploadPart`×N/`CompleteMultipartUpload` calls, then downloads and verifies byte-for-byte + HMAC + plaintext-SHA, reusing the existing `Monitor`'s verification logic rather than duplicating it. Surface a distinct `multipart_healthy` status alongside the existing canary status in `/armor/canary` and Prometheus metrics, so a multipart-specific regression is visible independently of the small-object check staying green.\n27\t2. **Strengthen `TestMultipartUpload` (and add sibling tests) to verify actual content, not just length.** Read the downloaded object back and compare bytes against what was uploaded per-part (e.g., seed each part with distinguishable content, not just a single marker byte). Add a variant that also exercises a non-multiple-of-part-size final part and a single-part multipart upload (edge cases the current test doesn't cover). Confirm this test actually gates the build pipeline that produces deployed images — a real content-correctness bug should never be able to ship.\n28\t3. **Add a lightweight version-drift check across known ARMOR deployments.** A scheduled check (can live in this repo or as an ops script) that reads each cluster's deployed ARMOR image tag from `declarative-config` and compares against the latest GitHub release, flagging any deployment more than N releases or M days behind — and flagging correctness-labeled releases distinctly from routine bumps, since those are the ones where staying behind is actually dangerous.\n29\t4. **Document a fix-propagation checklist.** Any commit fixing a correctness/data-integrity bug must enumerate every known ARMOR deployment (see the cluster list in `docs/disaster-recovery.md` / declarative-config) and confirm each is either patched or explicitly tracked as pending — before the fix is considered resolved, not just merged.\n30\t\n31\t## Consequences\n32\t\n33\t- Canary checks get more expensive (a multipart check involves more round-trips than the existing single-object check) — mitigated by running it on a longer interval than the existing 5-minute check.\n34\t- The version-drift check and propagation checklist are process/tooling additions, not core ARMOR features — they can live alongside the codebase without changing ARMOR's own request-handling surface.\n35\t- This does not retroactively recover any data corrupted before the fix was deployed everywhere — see `docs/disaster-recovery.md` for what's recoverable. It only prevents a similar regression from running undetected for weeks next time.\n36\t\n37\tRelated: `docs/disaster-recovery.md`, project memory `reference_armor_multipart_corruption_bug`, `project_commitgraph_split`.\n38\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_05401c11a1394c8dbaf12372","session_id":"805a8815-63d6-4b7a-adda-ceb49c557c44","uuid":"66df7124-d74e-4355-bc85-0d673cf35647","timestamp":"2026-07-15T12:24:08.842Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find multipart upload code"}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null},"usage":{"input_tokens":68,"output_tokens":43,"cache_read_input_tokens":37504,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard"}},"session_id":"805a8815-63d6-4b7a-adda-ceb49c557c44","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"6ec8442f-296d-445d-bc3f-7f3d33baa10c"}
{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_stop"},"session_id":"805a8815-63d6-4b7a-adda-ceb49c557c44","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"9f9d7514-78ee-4ce9-92a7-8fd7a3dac373"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e033bfb0616c47778bb6b3af","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ADR-002: Close detection gaps that let the multipart-upload corruption bug run 40 days undetected\n2\t\n3\t**Status:** Accepted\n4\t**Date:** 2026-07-11\n5\t\n6\t## Context\n7\t\n8\tCommits `b96d7eb`, `3a1526a`, and `c66e264` (landed 2026-06-10/11, versions 0.1.35–~0.1.41) fixed a real correctness bug: ARMOR only routed multipart-upload `POST` calls; a standard client's `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` fell through to plain `PutObject`. Each part got stored as if it were the whole object (\"last part wins\") while B2's real multipart upload never completed. Any object ≥5MiB written through an affected ARMOR instance via multipart semantics was silently corrupted — the write reported success, but the stored bytes were wrong.\n9\t\n10\t`ord-devimprint`'s ARMOR deployment was on `0.1.19` (last bumped 2026-05-31, before the fix) and was never caught up. This was discovered 2026-07-10/11 while investigating why `queue-api`'s litestream backup couldn't be restored: the backup's only surviving snapshot didn't decode, and two more objects were corrupted live *during the investigation*. Net effect: **queue-api's B2 backup chain had no valid restore point for a ~40-day window, and this was never flagged by anything running in production.** Full incident trail in project memory (`project_commitgraph_split`).\n11\t\n12\tThis same fix had already been deployed to `iad-native-ads` after an earlier incident there (barman WAL archiving). `ord-devimprint` was simply missed in that remediation pass. Two more clusters (`iad-ci` at 0.1.24, `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager` at 0.1.13) are still on old versions and have not been checked.\n13\t\n14\t### Why existing safeguards didn't catch this\n15\t\n16\tARMOR already has real integrity infrastructure — this incident is not a design gap in the crypto/verification model, it's a **coverage** gap in what that infrastructure actually exercises:\n17\t\n18\t1. **The self-healing canary monitor** (`internal/canary`) has been running continuously in production since Phase 1, checking upload→download→decrypt→HMAC-verify→SHA-verify every 5 minutes, and has been reporting healthy the entire time. It uses a fixed `canarySize` of 1024 bytes (`internal/server/server.go:117`) and calls `backend.Put()` directly — a single-object write. **It structurally never exercises `CreateMultipartUpload`/`UploadPart`/`CompleteMultipartUpload`**, which is exactly where the bug lived. A monitor built to catch exactly this class of regression has a blind spot sized precisely to miss it.\n19\t2. **`TestMultipartUpload`** (`tests/integration/integration_test.go:525`) already does a real 3-part, 15MB multipart upload/download cycle — but only asserts `ContentLength` matches the expected size. It never reads back and compares the actual bytes against what was uploaded. A corruption that happens to preserve object length (or a test/CI setup where this particular test isn't gating deploys) can pass silently.\n20\t3. **No mechanism tracks version drift** across ARMOR's several cluster deployments. A correctness fix landed on `main` and reached one cluster after a prior incident; nothing flagged that three other clusters (`ord-devimprint`, `iad-ci`, `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager`) were still running the vulnerable version.\n21\t\n22\t## Decision\n23\t\n24\tClose all three gaps. This ADR does **not** propose changes to the encryption/HMAC/provenance design — that infrastructure is sound for correctly-routed requests. It closes the coverage and operational gaps that let a routing regression run undetected.\n25\t\n26\t1. **Extend the canary monitor to cover the multipart path.** Add a second, longer-interval check (e.g., once per hour, alongside the existing 5-minute small-object check) that uploads a canary object sized just above the multipart threshold via real `CreateMultipartUpload`/`UploadPart`×N/`CompleteMultipartUpload` calls, then downloads and verifies byte-for-byte + HMAC + plaintext-SHA, reusing the existing `Monitor`'s verification logic rather than duplicating it. Surface a distinct `multipart_healthy` status alongside the existing canary status in `/armor/canary` and Prometheus metrics, so a multipart-specific regression is visible independently of the small-object check staying green.\n27\t2. **Strengthen `TestMultipartUpload` (and add sibling tests) to verify actual content, not just length.** Read the downloaded object back and compare bytes against what was uploaded per-part (e.g., seed each part with distinguishable content, not just a single marker byte). Add a variant that also exercises a non-multiple-of-part-size final part and a single-part multipart upload (edge cases the current test doesn't cover). Confirm this test actually gates the build pipeline that produces deployed images — a real content-correctness bug should never be able to ship.\n28\t3. **Add a lightweight version-drift check across known ARMOR deployments.** A scheduled check (can live in this repo or as an ops script) that reads each cluster's deployed ARMOR image tag from `declarative-config` and compares against the latest GitHub release, flagging any deployment more than N releases or M days behind — and flagging correctness-labeled releases distinctly from routine bumps, since those are the ones where staying behind is actually dangerous.\n29\t4. **Document a fix-propagation checklist.** Any commit fixing a correctness/data-integrity bug must enumerate every known ARMOR deployment (see the cluster list in `docs/disaster-recovery.md` / declarative-config) and confirm each is either patched or explicitly tracked as pending — before the fix is considered resolved, not just merged.\n30\t\n31\t## Consequences\n32\t\n33\t- Canary checks get more expensive (a multipart check involves more round-trips than the existing single-object check) — mitigated by running it on a longer interval than the existing 5-minute check.\n34\t- The version-drift check and propagation checklist are process/tooling additions, not core ARMOR features — they can live alongside the codebase without changing ARMOR's own request-handling surface.\n35\t- This does not retroactively recover any data corrupted before the fix was deployed everywhere — see `docs/disaster-recovery.md` for what's recoverable. It only prevents a similar regression from running undetected for weeks next time.\n36\t\n37\tRelated: `docs/disaster-recovery.md`, project memory `reference_armor_multipart_corruption_bug`, `project_commitgraph_split`.\n38\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"805a8815-63d6-4b7a-adda-ceb49c557c44","uuid":"154a4c4c-4aac-4ca7-92de-740c695ebf59","timestamp":"2026-07-15T12:24:23.098Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/adr/002-multipart-corruption-detection-gaps.md","content":"# ADR-002: Close detection gaps that let the multipart-upload corruption bug run 40 days undetected\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-07-11\n\n## Context\n\nCommits `b96d7eb`, `3a1526a`, and `c66e264` (landed 2026-06-10/11, versions 0.1.35–~0.1.41) fixed a real correctness bug: ARMOR only routed multipart-upload `POST` calls; a standard client's `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` fell through to plain `PutObject`. Each part got stored as if it were the whole object (\"last part wins\") while B2's real multipart upload never completed. Any object ≥5MiB written through an affected ARMOR instance via multipart semantics was silently corrupted — the write reported success, but the stored bytes were wrong.\n\n`ord-devimprint`'s ARMOR deployment was on `0.1.19` (last bumped 2026-05-31, before the fix) and was never caught up. This was discovered 2026-07-10/11 while investigating why `queue-api`'s litestream backup couldn't be restored: the backup's only surviving snapshot didn't decode, and two more objects were corrupted live *during the investigation*. Net effect: **queue-api's B2 backup chain had no valid restore point for a ~40-day window, and this was never flagged by anything running in production.** Full incident trail in project memory (`project_commitgraph_split`).\n\nThis same fix had already been deployed to `iad-native-ads` after an earlier incident there (barman WAL archiving). `ord-devimprint` was simply missed in that remediation pass. Two more clusters (`iad-ci` at 0.1.24, `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager` at 0.1.13) are still on old versions and have not been checked.\n\n### Why existing safeguards didn't catch this\n\nARMOR already has real integrity infrastructure — this incident is not a design gap in the crypto/verification model, it's a **coverage** gap in what that infrastructure actually exercises:\n\n1. **The self-healing canary monitor** (`internal/canary`) has been running continuously in production since Phase 1, checking upload→download→decrypt→HMAC-verify→SHA-verify every 5 minutes, and has been reporting healthy the entire time. It uses a fixed `canarySize` of 1024 bytes (`internal/server/server.go:117`) and calls `backend.Put()` directly — a single-object write. **It structurally never exercises `CreateMultipartUpload`/`UploadPart`/`CompleteMultipartUpload`**, which is exactly where the bug lived. A monitor built to catch exactly this class of regression has a blind spot sized precisely to miss it.\n2. **`TestMultipartUpload`** (`tests/integration/integration_test.go:525`) already does a real 3-part, 15MB multipart upload/download cycle — but only asserts `ContentLength` matches the expected size. It never reads back and compares the actual bytes against what was uploaded. A corruption that happens to preserve object length (or a test/CI setup where this particular test isn't gating deploys) can pass silently.\n3. **No mechanism tracks version drift** across ARMOR's several cluster deployments. A correctness fix landed on `main` and reached one cluster after a prior incident; nothing flagged that three other clusters (`ord-devimprint`, `iad-ci`, `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager`) were still running the vulnerable version.\n\n## Decision\n\nClose all three gaps. This ADR does **not** propose changes to the encryption/HMAC/provenance design — that infrastructure is sound for correctly-routed requests. It closes the coverage and operational gaps that let a routing regression run undetected.\n\n1. **Extend the canary monitor to cover the multipart path.** Add a second, longer-interval check (e.g., once per hour, alongside the existing 5-minute small-object check) that uploads a canary object sized just above the multipart threshold via real `CreateMultipartUpload`/`UploadPart`×N/`CompleteMultipartUpload` calls, then downloads and verifies byte-for-byte + HMAC + plaintext-SHA, reusing the existing `Monitor`'s verification logic rather than duplicating it. Surface a distinct `multipart_healthy` status alongside the existing canary status in `/armor/canary` and Prometheus metrics, so a multipart-specific regression is visible independently of the small-object check staying green.\n2. **Strengthen `TestMultipartUpload` (and add sibling tests) to verify actual content, not just length.** Read the downloaded object back and compare bytes against what was uploaded per-part (e.g., seed each part with distinguishable content, not just a single marker byte). Add a variant that also exercises a non-multiple-of-part-size final part and a single-part multipart upload (edge cases the current test doesn't cover). Confirm this test actually gates the build pipeline that produces deployed images — a real content-correctness bug should never be able to ship.\n3. **Add a lightweight version-drift check across known ARMOR deployments.** A scheduled check (can live in this repo or as an ops script) that reads each cluster's deployed ARMOR image tag from `declarative-config` and compares against the latest GitHub release, flagging any deployment more than N releases or M days behind — and flagging correctness-labeled releases distinctly from routine bumps, since those are the ones where staying behind is actually dangerous.\n4. **Document a fix-propagation checklist.** Any commit fixing a correctness/data-integrity bug must enumerate every known ARMOR deployment (see the cluster list in `docs/disaster-recovery.md` / declarative-config) and confirm each is either patched or explicitly tracked as pending — before the fix is considered resolved, not just merged.\n\n## Consequences\n\n- Canary checks get more expensive (a multipart check involves more round-trips than the existing single-object check) — mitigated by running it on a longer interval than the existing 5-minute check.\n- The version-drift check and propagation checklist are process/tooling additions, not core ARMOR features — they can live alongside the codebase without changing ARMOR's own request-handling surface.\n- This does not retroactively recover any data corrupted before the fix was deployed everywhere — see `docs/disaster-recovery.md` for what's recoverable. It only prevents a similar regression from running undetected for weeks next time.\n\nRelated: `docs/disaster-recovery.md`, project memory `reference_armor_multipart_corruption_bug`, `project_commitgraph_split`.\n","numLines":38,"startLine":1,"totalLines":38}}}

streaming encryption.\\n411\\t// It uses a temp file to avoid loading the entire plaintext into memory.\\n412\\t// The process is:\\n413\\t// 1. {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Split concatenated HMACs into individual HMACs for storage\n\t// The i++ {\n\t\tblockHMACs[i] = blockHMACsRaw[i*crypto.HMACSize : (i+1)*crypto.HMACSize]\n\t}\n\n\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n\n\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\nfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn","numLines":120,"startLine":2029,"totalLines":2802}}}

Multipart objects are raw concatenated part ciphertext with the HMAC table in a *sidecar*, so every GET 500s (\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: offset out of range\"). Found by the new full-cycle test on its first run. Likely a second contributor to the ADR-002 \"snapshot didn't decode\" incident, independent of the routing bug.\n2. **`bf-1v2ehf` (P2): multipart objects store a placeholder plaintext-SHA.** `CompleteMultipartUpload` sets `plaintextSHA = sha256(\"\")` (`handlers.go:2132-2134`) — the manifest/provenance SHA for every multipart object is the empty hash; real corruption can't be distinguished from the placeholder.\n3. **`bf-59unr3` (P1): U6/U7/U8 are design-level suspects, not regression risks.** The per-part CTR offset scheme assumes strictly sequential, exactly-once, block-aligned parts. Real SDKs violate all three by default. Until fixed or explicitly rejected (400 rather than corrupt), **clients writing through ARMOR must be pinned to sequential, block-aligned, no-retry part behavior — including the commitgraph clone-workers.**\n4. **`bf-15sdaf` (P1): `internal/server` does not compile on main** (redeclared helpers from the recent bead-thrash commits), blocking the whole-module build and the new L1 chunked-decoder test.\n5. Consumers on pre-fix ARMOR versions: per ADR-002, `iad-ci` (0.1.24) and `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager` (0.1.13) remain on multipart-corrupting builds. `ord-devimprint` is patched (0.1.42, verified 2026-07-15).\n\n**Consequence for the commitgraph rebuild:** until `bf-24sxh7` and `bf-59unr3` are fixed *and* the multipart canary is deployed to the target cluster, ARMOR multipart cannot be certified for the corpus write path. Interim options: single-PUT streaming writes only (stay under the SDK multipart cutover), or direct-B2 writes with ARMOR reserved for delivery.\n\n## Test-run cheat sheet\n\n```\ngo test ./internal/server/...          # L1 + L3 (routing tripwire, full-cycle, chunked decode)\ngo test ./internal/backend/...         # L2 multipart assembly byte-verification\ncurl $ARMOR/armor/canary               # L4: small + multipart canary status in prod\n```","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_05401c11a1394c8dbaf12372","session_id":"805a8815-63d6-4b7a-adda-ceb49c557c44","uuid":"90c219ca-0d69-47a9-8210-02bac4714901","timestamp":"2026-07-15T12:24:45.452Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find multipart upload code"}

It mirrors manifest.Entry but is defined here to avoid an\n// import cycle between the handlers and manifest packages.\ntype ManifestEntry struct {\n\tPlaintextSize int64\n\tContentType   string\n\tETag          string\n\tLastModified  time.Time\n\tIV            []byte\n\tWrappedDEK    []byte\n\tBlockSize     int\n}\n\n// ManifestRecorder records successful S3 write operations in the manifest\n// index for fast in-memory lookup and enqueues them for async B2 persistence\n// as delta files. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.\ntype ManifestRecorder interface {\n\t// RecordPut records a successful PutObject or CompleteMultipartUpload.\n\tRecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string)\n\t// RecordDelete records a successful DeleteObject.\n\tRecordDelete(bucket, key string)\n\t// Lookup returns manifest metadata for bucket/key, or (nil, false) if not\n\t// tracked. Used to serve HeadObject and ListObjectVersions from memory\n\t// without a B2 round-trip.\n\tLookup(bucket, key string) (*ManifestEntry, bool)\n}\n\n// Handlers contains all S3 operation handlers.\ntype Handlers struct {\n\tconfig      *config.Config\n\tbackend     backend.Backend\n\tcache       *backend.MetadataCache\n\tfooterCache *backend.FooterCache\n\tlistCache   *backend.ListCache\n\tkeyManager  *keymanager.KeyManager\n\tprovenance  ProvenanceRecorder\n\tmanifest    ManifestRecorder\n}\n\n// New creates a new Handlers instance.\nfunc New(cfg *config.Config, be backend.Backend, cache *backend.MetadataCache, footerCache *backend.FooterCache, km *keymanager.KeyManager, listCache *backend.ListCache) *Handlers {\n\treturn &Handlers{\n\t\tconfig:      cfg,\n\t\tbackend:     be,\n\t\tcache:       cache,\n\t\tfooterCache: footerCache,\n\t\tlistCache:   listCache,\n\t\tkeyManager:  km,\n\t\tprovenance:  nil,\n\t\tmanifest:    nil,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithProvenance adds provenance support to handlers.\nfunc (h *Handlers) WithProvenance(p ProvenanceRecorder) {\n\th.provenance = p\n}\n\n// WithManifest wires a ManifestRecorder into the handlers so that successful\n// PutObject and DeleteObject calls update the in-memory index and enqueue\n// delta ops for async B2 persistence.\nfunc (h *Handlers) WithManifest(m ManifestRecorder) {\n\th.manifest = m\n}\n\n// HandleRoot routes S3 operations based on the request.\nfunc (h *Handlers) HandleRoot(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tpath := r.URL.Path\n\n\t// Parse bucket and key from path\n\t// Path format: /bucket/key or /bucket\n\tpath = strings.TrimPrefix(path, \"/\")\n\tparts := strings.SplitN(path, \"/\", 2)\n\n\tbucket := \"\"\n\tkey := \"\"\n\tif len(parts) > 0 && parts[0] != \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = parts[0]\n\t}\n\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n\t\tkey = parts[1]\n\t\t// URL decode the key (DuckDB httpfs encodes special chars like = as %3D)\n\t\tif decoded, err := url.PathUnescape(key); err == nil {\n\t\t\tkey = decoded\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Protect the .armor/ reserved namespace\n\t// Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return 403 AccessDenied\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(key, \".armor/\") {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access to .armor/ reserved namespace is denied\", 403)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Route based on method and path\n\tswitch r.Method {\n\tcase http.MethodGet:\n\t\t// Handle ListMultipartUploads (GET ?uploads on bucket, no key)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"uploads\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.ListMultipartUploads(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle ListObjectVersions (GET ?versions on bucket)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"versions\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.ListObjectVersions(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration (GET ?lifecycle on bucket)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"lifecycle\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle GetObjectLockConfiguration (GET ?object-lock on bucket)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"object-lock\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.GetObjectLockConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle GetObjectRetention (GET ?retention on object)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"retention\") && key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.GetObjectRetention(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle GetObjectLegalHold (GET ?legal-hold on object)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"legal-hold\") && key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.GetObjectLegalHold(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle ListParts (GET ?uploadId on object)\n\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.ListParts(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Regular Get operations\n\t\tif key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.GetObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\tq := r.URL.Query()\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase q.Has(\"location\"):\n\t\t\t\th.GetBucketLocation(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\tcase q.Has(\"versioning\"):\n\t\t\t\th.GetBucketVersioning(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// All other bucket-level GETs are list operations.\n\t\t\t\t// HeadBucket is only for HTTP HEAD method (handled in MethodHead case).\n\t\t\t\th.ListObjectsV2(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\th.ListBuckets(w, r)\n\t\t}\n\tcase http.MethodPut:\n\t\t// Handle PutBucketLifecycleConfiguration (PUT ?lifecycle on bucket)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"lifecycle\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.PutBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle PutObjectLockConfiguration (PUT ?object-lock on bucket)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"object-lock\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.PutObjectLockConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle PutObjectRetention (PUT ?retention on object)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"retention\") && key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.PutObjectRetention(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle PutObjectLegalHold (PUT ?legal-hold on object)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"legal-hold\") && key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.PutObjectLegalHold(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif key != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Handle UploadPart (PUT ?partNumber&uploadId on object) — standard\n\t\t\t// S3 clients PUT parts; routing this to PutObject silently stored\n\t\t\t// each part as the whole object and left the B2 multipart upload\n\t\t\t// empty, so CompleteMultipartUpload always failed with InvalidPart.\n\t\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check for CopyObject (has x-amz-copy-source header)\n\t\t\tif r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\") != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\th.CopyObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\th.PutObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.CreateBucket(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t}\n\tcase http.MethodHead:\n\t\tif key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.HeadObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.HeadBucket(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t}\n\tcase http.MethodDelete:\n\t\t// Handle DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration (DELETE ?lifecycle on bucket)\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"lifecycle\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Handle AbortMultipartUpload (DELETE ?uploadId on object)\n\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.AbortMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif key != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.DeleteObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t\th.DeleteBucket(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t}\n\tcase http.MethodPost:\n\t\t// Handle multipart upload operations\n\t\tuploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\")\n\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"uploads\") {\n\t\t\th.CreateMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\t} else if uploadID != \"\" {\n\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\th.CompleteMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if r.URL.Query().Has(\"delete\") {\n\t\t\t// DeleteObjects (bulk delete) - uses POST with ?delete query param\n\t\t\th.DeleteObjects(w, r, bucket)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Unsupported POST operation\", 400)\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"MethodNotAllowed\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Method %s not allowed\", r.Method), 405)\n\t}\n}\n\n// PutObject handles S3 PutObject with encryption.\n// For small files (<10MB), it buffers in memory.\n// For larger files, it uses streaming encryption via temp files to avoid\n// loading the entire file into memory.\nfunc (h *Handlers) PutObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Check Content-Length header\n\tcontentLength := r.ContentLength\n\tstreamingThreshold := int64(10 * 1024 * 1024) // 10MB threshold\n\n\t// Use streaming for large files or unknown size\n\tuseStreaming := contentLength < 0 || contentLength > streamingThreshold\n\n\tif useStreaming {\n\t\th.putObjectStreaming(ctx, w, r, bucket, key)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Small file: buffer in memory\n\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n\n\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(plaintext)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt data\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope: header + encrypted blocks + HMAC table\n\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + int64(len(encrypted)) + int64(len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, envelopeSize)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute plaintext ETag (MD5)\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(plaintext)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n\t}\n\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n\t}\n\n\t// Record provenance\n\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n\tif h.listCache != nil {\n\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Return ETag\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// putObjectStreaming handles large file uploads with streaming encryption.\n// It uses a temp file to avoid loading the entire plaintext into memory.\n// The process is:\n// 1. Stream request body to temp file while computing SHA-256\n// 2. Create envelope header with the computed SHA-256\n// 3. Stream from temp file through encryption to B2 via io.Pipe\n// 4. Clean up temp file\nfunc (h *Handlers) putObjectStreaming(ctx context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\t// Phase 1: Stream to temp file and compute SHA-256\n\ttmpFile, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-upload-*.tmp\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ttmpPath := tmpFile.Name()\n\tdefer os.Remove(tmpPath) // Clean up on exit\n\n\t// Compute SHA-256 while copying to temp file\n\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n\tteeReader := io.TeeReader(r.Body, plaintextHash)\n\n\tplaintextSize, err := io.Copy(tmpFile, teeReader)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the computed SHA-256\n\tvar plaintextSHA [32]byte\n\tcopy(plaintextSHA[:], plaintextHash.Sum(nil))\n\n\t// Seek back to beginning of temp file for reading\n\tif _, err := tmpFile.Seek(0, 0); err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to seek temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 2: Get encryption keys\n\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create encryptor\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate envelope size\n\tblockCount := crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize)\n\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + plaintextSize + hmacTableSize\n\n\t// Phase 3: Stream encrypt via io.Pipe\n\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n\n\t// Start encryption goroutine\n\tencErr := make(chan error, 1)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n\t\tdefer close(encErr)\n\n\t\t// Write header\n\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(headerBytes); err != nil {\n\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write header: %w\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Stream encrypt the plaintext\n\t\thmacTable, err := encryptor.EncryptStream(tmpFile, pw, plaintextSize)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"encryption failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Write HMAC table\n\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(hmacTable); err != nil {\n\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tencErr <- nil\n\t}()\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute ETag while we have the temp file\n\t// We need to read the file again for MD5, but we already have SHA-256\n\t// Use SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes as ETag for streaming (non-standard but works)\n\tetag := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:16])\n\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied using streaming reader\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, pr, envelopeSize, meta); err != nil {\n\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n\t\t// Check if there was an encryption error\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase encErrVal := <-encErr:\n\t\t\tif encErrVal != nil {\n\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Close temp file\n\ttmpFile.Close()\n\n\t// Check for encryption errors\n\tif encErrVal := <-encErr; encErrVal != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n\t}\n\n\t// Record provenance\n\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put-streaming\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n\tif h.listCache != nil {\n\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Return ETag\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Streaming\", \"true\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// GetObject handles S3 GetObject with decryption and range support.\nfunc (h *Handlers) GetObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\n\t// Get metadata first\n\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\t// Check conditional request headers for non-ARMOR objects\n\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, info.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Passthrough for non-ARMOR objects\n\t\tbody, _, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(info.Size, 10))\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this object using the key ID from metadata\n\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create decryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Check conditional request headers\n\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, armorMeta.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n\t\t\t// 304 Not Modified - set headers but no body\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// 412 Precondition Failed\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if this is a multipart object (HMAC table in sidecar, no embedded header)\n\tisMultipart := info.Metadata[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] == \"true\"\n\n\t// Check for range request\n\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n\t\th.handleRangeRequest(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified, isMultipart)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Full object download with pipelined stream decryption\n\th.handleFullObjectStream(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified, isMultipart)\n}\n\n// handleFullObjectStream handles full object downloads with pipelined stream decryption.\n// This uses io.Pipe to decrypt blocks as they stream from Cloudflare, reducing\n// time-to-first-byte and memory usage compared to buffering the entire envelope.\nfunc (h *Handlers) handleFullObjectStream(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time, isMultipart bool) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\n\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\n\n\tvar hmacTable []byte\n\tvar dataBody io.ReadCloser\n\tvar streamSize int64\n\tvar header *crypto.EnvelopeHeader\n\n\tif isMultipart {\n\t\t// Multipart object: HMAC table is in sidecar, no embedded header\n\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\t\tsidecar, err := manager.LoadHMACTable(ctx, key)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load HMAC table from sidecar: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Concatenate all block HMACs from sidecar\n\t\tfor _, hmac := range sidecar.BlockHMACs {\n\t\t\thmacTable = append(hmacTable, hmac...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Data starts at offset 0 (no header), read only the encrypted data\n\t\tstreamSize = plaintextSize\n\t\tdataBody, err = h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Single-PUT object: embedded HMAC table at end of file\n\t\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n\t\tdataSize := plaintextSize\n\n\t\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table (small range read)\n\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\t\thmacBody.Close()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// 2. Start streaming data from Cloudflare (header + encrypted blocks, stop before HMAC)\n\t\tstreamSize = crypto.HeaderSize + dataSize\n\t\tdataBody, err = h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n\n\t\t// 3. Read and discard the 64-byte header (single-PUT only)\n\t\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\t\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Parse header to get plaintext SHA for verification\n\t\theader, err = crypto.DecodeHeader(headerBuf)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode header: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Set response headers before streaming\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(plaintextSize, 10))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Stream\", \"pipelined\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\n\t// 5. Stream decrypt using io.Pipe\n\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n\n\t// Start decryption goroutine\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n\n\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n\t\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n\n\t\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n\t\t\t// Calculate actual block size (last block may be smaller)\n\t\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n\t\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n\n\t\t\t// Read encrypted block\n\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n\t\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n\t\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"read error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif n == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n\n\t\t\t// Verify HMAC\n\t\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n\t\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC table too short at block %d\", blockIndex))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n\n\t\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n\t\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n\t\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n\t\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n\t\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n\n\t\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"block %d: HMAC verification failed\", blockIndex))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Decrypt block (need to use CTR stream)\n\t\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n\t\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n\t\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n\n\t\t\t// Update plaintext hash for verification\n\t\t\tplaintextHash.Write(decrypted)\n\n\t\t\t// Write plaintext to pipe\n\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(decrypted); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"write error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify plaintext SHA-256\n\t\tcomputedSHA := plaintextHash.Sum(nil)\n\t\tvar expectedSHA []byte\n\t\tif isMultipart {\n\t\t\t// For multipart objects, use SHA from metadata (placeholder until tracked during upload)\n\t\t\t// See bf-1v2ehf: multipart currently stores empty hash\n\t\t\texpectedSHA, _ = hex.DecodeString(armorMeta.PlaintextSHA)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// For single-PUT objects, use SHA from header\n\t\t\texpectedSHA = header.PlaintextSHA[:]\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(expectedSHA) > 0 && !bytes.Equal(computedSHA, expectedSHA) {\n\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA-256 mismatch\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\t// Stream plaintext to client\n\tio.Copy(w, pr)\n}\n\n// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\nfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n\treturn counter\n}\n\nfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// handleRangeRequest handles range read requests.\nfunc (h *Handlers) handleRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time, isMultipart bool) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\n\t// Parse range header (bytes=start-end)\n\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRange\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid range: %v\", err), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if this is a Parquet footer request and we have it cached\n\t// DuckDB reads footer in two steps: last 8 bytes, then footer body\n\t// Footer is at the end of the file: [footer_metadata][footer_length (4B)][PAR1 (4B)]\n\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 {\n\t\t// This range includes the end of the file - could be a footer read\n\t\tif footer, ok := h.footerCache.Get(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag); ok {\n\t\t\t// We have a cached footer, serve from cache\n\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(footer))\n\t\t\tif start >= footerStart {\n\t\t\t\t// Request is entirely within the cached footer\n\t\t\t\toffset := start - footerStart\n\t\t\t\tfooterEnd := offset + (end - start + 1)\n\t\t\t\tif footerEnd <= int64(len(footer)) {\n\t\t\t\t\tplaintext := footer[offset:footerEnd]\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Footer-Cache\", \"HIT\")\n\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n\tvar startBlockIndex int\n\n\tif isMultipart {\n\t\t// Multipart object: load HMAC table from sidecar, no embedded header\n\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\t\tsidecar, err := manager.LoadHMACTable(ctx, key)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load HMAC table from sidecar: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Concatenate all block HMACs from sidecar\n\t\tfor _, hmac := range sidecar.BlockHMACs {\n\t\t\thmacTable = append(hmacTable, hmac...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Calculate block range for this request\n\t\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n\t\tstartBlockIndex = int(start / int64(blockSize))\n\t\tendBlockIndex := int(end / int64(blockSize))\n\n\t\t// Clamp to valid range\n\t\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\n\t\tif endBlockIndex >= blockCount {\n\t\t\tendBlockIndex = blockCount - 1\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Calculate encrypted data range (no header offset for multipart)\n\t\tdataOffset := int64(startBlockIndex * blockSize)\n\t\tlastBlockEnd := int64((endBlockIndex + 1) * blockSize)\n\t\tif lastBlockEnd > plaintextSize {\n\t\t\tlastBlockEnd = plaintextSize\n\t\t}\n\t\tdataLength := lastBlockEnd - dataOffset\n\n\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks\n\t\tencryptedBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, dataOffset, dataLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n\n\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Single-PUT object: embedded HMAC table at end of file\n\t\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n\t\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel using errgroup.\n\t\t// This cuts range-read latency nearly in half for cache misses.\n\t\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\n\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\t\tencryptedBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n\n\t\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n\n\t\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", err.Error(), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Decrypt range (DecryptRange internally calculates block indices from plaintext offsets)\n\t// For multipart objects, hmacTableIsFull=true (all HMACs from sidecar).\n\t// For single-PUT objects, hmacTableIsFull=false (only range HMACs from embedded table).\n\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize, isMultipart)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Cache Parquet footer if this looks like a footer read\n\t// Footer is detected by: 1) Reading near end of file, 2) Data ends with \"PAR1\" magic\n\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 && len(plaintext) >= 8 {\n\t\tif backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n\t\t\t// This is a Parquet file, try to cache the full footer\n\t\t\t// If we just read the last 8 bytes, cache it for footer length detection\n\t\t\t// If we read more, it might be the full footer\n\t\t\th.cacheParquetFooter(ctx, bucket, key, armorMeta, plaintext, plaintextSize)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n\tw.Write(plaintext)\n}\n\n// cacheParquetFooter caches Parquet footer data for faster subsequent reads.\nfunc (h *Handlers) cacheParquetFooter(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintext []byte, plaintextSize int64) {\n\t// If we got the last 8 bytes, we can determine footer length\n\tif len(plaintext) == 8 {\n\t\t// This is the footer length read - cache a small marker\n\t\t// The actual footer will be cached on the next read\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if we have the full footer by verifying PAR1 magic at end\n\tif len(plaintext) >= 8 && backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n\t\t// We have at least part of the footer\n\t\t// Parse footer length from the last 8 bytes if available\n\t\tfooterRange, err := backend.GetParquetFooterRange(plaintext[len(plaintext)-8:], plaintextSize)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if we have the complete footer\n\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(footerRange.Length) - 8\n\t\trequestStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(plaintext))\n\n\t\tif requestStart <= footerStart {\n\t\t\t// We have the complete footer, extract and cache it\n\t\t\tfooterOffset := footerStart - requestStart\n\t\t\tif footerOffset >= 0 && int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8 <= len(plaintext) {\n\t\t\t\tfooter := plaintext[footerOffset : int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8]\n\t\t\t\th.footerCache.Set(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag, footer)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkConditionalRequest evaluates conditional headers and returns the appropriate\n// response status. Returns 0 if the request should proceed normally.\n// Supports: If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\nfunc checkConditionalRequest(r *http.Request, etag string, lastModified time.Time) int {\n\tifMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-Match\")\n\tifNoneMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-None-Match\")\n\tifModifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Modified-Since\")\n\tifUnmodifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Unmodified-Since\")\n\n\t// Normalize ETag (remove quotes if present for comparison)\n\tnormalizedETag := strings.Trim(etag, `\"`)\n\n\t// If-Match: Return 412 Precondition Failed if ETag doesn't match\n\tif ifMatch != \"\" {\n\t\t// If-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n\t\tif ifMatch == \"*\" {\n\t\t\t// Match any existing resource - proceed\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifMatch, \",\")\n\t\t\tmatched := false\n\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !matched {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If-Unmodified-Since: Return 412 Precondition Failed if modified since date\n\tif ifUnmodifiedSince != \"\" {\n\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifUnmodifiedSince); err == nil {\n\t\t\tif lastModified.After(t) {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If-None-Match: Return 304 Not Modified if ETag matches\n\tif ifNoneMatch != \"\" {\n\t\t// If-None-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n\t\tif ifNoneMatch == \"*\" {\n\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifNoneMatch, \",\")\n\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If-Modified-Since: Return 304 Not Modified if not modified since date\n\t// Only applies if If-None-Match is not present (per RFC 7232)\n\tif ifModifiedSince != \"\" && ifNoneMatch == \"\" {\n\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifModifiedSince); err == nil {\n\t\t\t// Use >= comparison per RFC 7232\n\t\t\tif !lastModified.After(t) {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\nfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n\n\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t} else {\n\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Clamp end per RFC 7233: a range ending beyond the file size is satisfiable\n\t// as long as start is within bounds. DuckDB httpfs requests aligned blocks\n\t// (e.g. bytes=0-131071) regardless of actual file size.\n\tif end >= totalSize {\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t}\n\n\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn start, end, nil\n}\n\n// HeadObject handles S3 HeadObject.\nfunc (h *Handlers) HeadObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Fast path: serve from the in-memory manifest index when available,\n\t// avoiding a B2 HeadObject round-trip entirely.\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, key); ok {\n\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, entry.ETag, entry.LastModified); status != 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(entry.PlaintextSize, 10))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", entry.ContentType)\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Manifest miss or disabled: fall back to a B2 HeadObject call.\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Object not found\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Determine ETag and content info based on encryption status\n\tvar etag string\n\tvar contentLength int64\n\tvar contentType string\n\tif info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n\t\t\tetag = am.ETag\n\t\t\tcontentLength = am.PlaintextSize\n\t\t\tcontentType = am.ContentType\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tetag = info.ETag\n\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n\t}\n\n\t// Check conditional request headers\n\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, etag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(contentLength, 10))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", contentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// DeleteObject handles S3 DeleteObject.\nfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\tif err := h.backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to delete: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove from manifest\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n\t}\n\n\t// Invalidate metadata cache and list cache\n\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n\tif h.listCache != nil {\n\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// CopyObject handles S3 CopyObject with DEK re-wrapping for ARMOR-encrypted objects.\n// This supports:\n// - Renaming files (same bucket, different key)\n// - Copying files (potentially different bucket)\n// - Key rotation (re-wraps DEK with current MEK)\nfunc (h *Handlers) CopyObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dstBucket, dstKey string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Parse copy source header\n\tcopySource := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\")\n\tif copySource == \"\" {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing x-amz-copy-source header\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse source bucket and key\n\t// Format: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n\tsrcBucket, srcKey := parseCopySource(copySource)\n\tif srcBucket == \"\" || srcKey == \"\" {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidCopySource\", \"Invalid copy source format\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n\tsrcPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(srcKey)\n\tdstPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(dstKey)\n\n\t// Get source object metadata\n\tsrcInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Source object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check metadata directive\n\tmetadataDirective := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-metadata-directive\")\n\treplaceMetadata := metadataDirective == \"REPLACE\"\n\n\t// Build response XML structure\n\ttype CopyObjectResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName      xml.Name `xml:\"CopyObjectResult\"`\n\t\tLastModified string   `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t\tETag         string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle ARMOR-encrypted objects\n\tif srcInfo.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(srcInfo.Metadata)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get the source MEK using the key ID from metadata\n\t\tsrcMEK, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get source decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Unwrap DEK with source MEK\n\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(srcMEK, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get the destination MEK for the target key\n\t\tdstMEK, dstKeyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(dstKey)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get destination encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Re-wrap DEK with destination MEK (handles key rotation and cross-prefix copy)\n\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(dstMEK, dek)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to re-wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Build new metadata with re-wrapped DEK and destination key ID\n\t\tnewMeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\t\tVersion:       armorMeta.Version,\n\t\t\tBlockSize:     armorMeta.BlockSize,\n\t\t\tPlaintextSize: armorMeta.PlaintextSize,\n\t\t\tContentType:   armorMeta.ContentType,\n\t\t\tIV:            armorMeta.IV,\n\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  armorMeta.PlaintextSHA,\n\t\t\tETag:          armorMeta.ETag,\n\t\t\tKeyID:         dstKeyID,\n\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t\t// Handle REPLACE directive - copy custom metadata headers from request\n\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n\t\t\t// Check for new content-type in request\n\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tnewMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-content-type\"] = ct\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Copy any additional custom headers from request\n\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") && !strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-Armor-\") {\n\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Perform server-side copy with updated metadata\n\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, newMeta, true); err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Record provenance for the copy\n\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(dstKey) {\n\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, dstKey, armorMeta.PlaintextSHA, \"copy\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Invalidate cache for destination\n\t\th.cache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n\t\th.footerCache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n\n\t\t// Return success response\n\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\t\tw.Write(output)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Non-ARMOR object - pass through copy\n\tvar meta map[string]string\n\tif replaceMetadata {\n\t\tmeta = make(map[string]string)\n\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmeta[\"Content-Type\"] = ct\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") {\n\t\t\t\tmeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, meta, replaceMetadata); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the destination object info for ETag\n\tdstInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to get destination info\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Return success response\n\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, dstInfo.ETag),\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// parseCopySource parses the x-amz-copy-source header value.\n// Supports formats: /bucket/key or bucket/key\nfunc parseCopySource(copySource string) (bucket, key string) {\n\t// Remove leading slash if present\n\tcopySource = strings.TrimPrefix(copySource, \"/\")\n\n\t// URL decode the key portion (keys may contain special characters)\n\tif idx := strings.Index(copySource, \"/\"); idx != -1 {\n\t\tbucket = copySource[:idx]\n\t\tkey = copySource[idx+1:]\n\t\t// URL decode the key\n\t\tif decoded, err := url.QueryUnescape(key); err == nil {\n\t\t\tkey = decoded\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn bucket, key\n}\n\n// enrichPlaintextSizes replaces encrypted on-disk sizes with plaintext sizes for\n// ARMOR-encrypted objects. B2's ListObjectsV2 response includes the encrypted\n// object size; we resolve the plaintext size using the metadata cache (if warm)\n// or a parallel HeadObject call per object (on cache miss).
