Returns 0 if the request should proceed normally.\n// Supports: If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\nfunc checkConditionalRequest(r *http.Request, etag string, lastModified time.Time) int {\n\tifMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-Match\")\n\tifNoneMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-None-Match\")\n\tifModifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Modified-Since\")\n\tifUnmodifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Unmodified-Since\")\n\n\t// Normalize ETag (remove quotes if present for comparison)\n\tnormalizedETag := strings.Trim(etag, `\"`)\n\n\t// If-Match: Return 412 Precondition Failed if ETag doesn't match\n\tif ifMatch != \"\" {\n\t\t// If-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n\t\tif ifMatch == \"*\" {\n\t\t\t// Match any existing resource - proceed\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifMatch, \",\")\n\t\t\tmatched := false\n\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !matched {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If-Unmodified-Since: Return 412 Precondition Failed if modified since date\n\tif ifUnmodifiedSince != \"\" {\n\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifUnmodifiedSince); err == nil {\n\t\t\tif lastModified.After(t) {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If-None-Match: Return 304 Not Modified if ETag matches\n\tif ifNoneMatch != \"\" {\n\t\t// If-None-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n\t\tif ifNoneMatch == \"*\" {\n\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifNoneMatch, \",\")\n\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If-Modified-Since: Return 304 Not Modified if not modified since date\n\t// Only applies if If-None-Match is not present (per RFC 7232)\n\tif ifModifiedSince != \"\" && ifNoneMatch == \"\" {\n\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifModifiedSince); err == nil {\n\t\t\t// Use >= comparison per RFC 7232\n\t\t\tif !lastModified.After(t) {\n\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\nfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n\n\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t} else {\n\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Clamp end per RFC 7233: a range ending beyond the file size is satisfiable\n\t// as long as start is within bounds. DuckDB httpfs requests aligned blocks\n\t// (e.g. bytes=0-131071) regardless of actual file size.\n\tif end >= totalSize {\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t}\n\n\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn start, end, nil\n}\n\n// HeadObject handles S3 HeadObject.\nfunc (h *Handlers) HeadObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Fast path: serve from the in-memory manifest index when available,\n\t// avoiding a B2 HeadObject round-trip entirely.\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, key); ok {\n\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, entry.ETag, entry.LastModified); status != 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(entry.PlaintextSize, 10))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", entry.ContentType)\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Manifest miss or disabled: fall back to a B2 HeadObject call.\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Object not found\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Determine ETag and content info based on encryption status\n\tvar etag string\n\tvar contentLength int64\n\tvar contentType string\n\tif info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n\t\t\tetag = am.ETag\n\t\t\tcontentLength = am.PlaintextSize\n\t\t\tcontentType = am.ContentType\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tetag = info.ETag\n\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n\t}\n\n\t// Check conditional request headers\n\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, etag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(contentLength, 10))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", contentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// DeleteObject handles S3 DeleteObject.\nfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n\tif err := h.backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to delete: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove from manifest\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n\t}\n\n\t// Invalidate metadata cache and list cache\n\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n\tif h.listCache != nil {\n\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// CopyObject handles S3 CopyObject with DEK re-wrapping for ARMOR-encrypted objects.\n// This supports:\n// - Renaming files (same bucket, different key)\n// - Copying files (potentially different bucket)\n// - Key rotation (re-wraps DEK with current MEK)\nfunc (h *Handlers) CopyObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dstBucket, dstKey string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Parse copy source header\n\tcopySource := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\")\n\tif copySource == \"\" {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing x-amz-copy-source header\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse source bucket and key\n\t// Format: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n\tsrcBucket, srcKey := parseCopySource(copySource)\n\tif srcBucket == \"\" || srcKey == \"\" {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidCopySource\", \"Invalid copy source format\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n\tsrcPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(srcKey)\n\tdstPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(dstKey)\n\n\t// Get source object metadata\n\tsrcInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Source object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check metadata directive\n\tmetadataDirective := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-metadata-directive\")\n\treplaceMetadata := metadataDirective == \"REPLACE\"\n\n\t// Build response XML structure\n\ttype CopyObjectResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName      xml.Name `xml:\"CopyObjectResult\"`\n\t\tLastModified string   `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t\tETag         string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle ARMOR-encrypted objects\n\tif srcInfo.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(srcInfo.Metadata)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get the source MEK using the key ID from metadata\n\t\tsrcMEK, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get source decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Unwrap DEK with source MEK\n\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(srcMEK, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get the destination MEK for the target key\n\t\tdstMEK, dstKeyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(dstKey)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get destination encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Re-wrap DEK with destination MEK (handles key rotation and cross-prefix copy)\n\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(dstMEK, dek)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to re-wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Build new metadata with re-wrapped DEK and destination key ID\n\t\tnewMeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\t\tVersion:       armorMeta.Version,\n\t\t\tBlockSize:     armorMeta.BlockSize,\n\t\t\tPlaintextSize: armorMeta.PlaintextSize,\n\t\t\tContentType:   armorMeta.ContentType,\n\t\t\tIV:            armorMeta.IV,\n\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  armorMeta.PlaintextSHA,\n\t\t\tETag:          armorMeta.ETag,\n\t\t\tKeyID:         dstKeyID,\n\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t\t// Handle REPLACE directive - copy custom metadata headers from request\n\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n\t\t\t// Check for new content-type in request\n\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tnewMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-content-type\"] = ct\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Copy any additional custom headers from request\n\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") && !strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-Armor-\") {\n\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Perform server-side copy with updated metadata\n\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, newMeta, true); err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Record provenance for the copy\n\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(dstKey) {\n\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, dstKey, armorMeta.PlaintextSHA, \"copy\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Invalidate cache for destination\n\t\th.cache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n\t\th.footerCache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n\n\t\t// Return success response\n\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\t\tw.Write(output)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Non-ARMOR object - pass through copy\n\tvar meta map[string]string\n\tif replaceMetadata {\n\t\tmeta = make(map[string]string)\n\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmeta[\"Content-Type\"] = ct\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") {\n\t\t\t\tmeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, meta, replaceMetadata); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the destination object info for ETag\n\tdstInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to get destination info\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Return success response\n\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, dstInfo.ETag),\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// parseCopySource parses the x-amz-copy-source header value.\n// Supports formats: /bucket/key or bucket/key\nfunc parseCopySource(copySource string) (bucket, key string) {\n\t// Remove leading slash if present\n\tcopySource = strings.TrimPrefix(copySource, \"/\")\n\n\t// URL decode the key portion (keys may contain special characters)\n\tif idx := strings.Index(copySource, \"/\"); idx != -1 {\n\t\tbucket = copySource[:idx]\n\t\tkey = copySource[idx+1:]\n\t\t// URL decode the key\n\t\tif decoded, err := url.QueryUnescape(key); err == nil {\n\t\t\tkey = decoded\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn bucket, key\n}\n\n// enrichPlaintextSizes replaces encrypted on-disk sizes with plaintext sizes for\n// ARMOR-encrypted objects. B2's ListObjectsV2 response includes the encrypted\n// object size; we resolve the plaintext size using the metadata cache (if warm)\n// or a parallel HeadObject call per object (on cache miss). Each errgroup goroutine\n// writes to a distinct slice index so no mutex is needed.\nfunc (h *Handlers) enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx context.Context, bucket string, result *backend.ListResult) {\n\teg, egCtx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\tfor i := range result.Objects {\n\t\ti := i\n\t\tkey := result.Objects[i].Key\n\t\tif am, ok := h.cache.Get(bucket, key); ok {\n\t\t\tif am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = am.PlaintextSize\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\teg.Go(func() error {\n\t\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(egCtx, bucket, key)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil // non-fatal: keep encrypted size rather than failing the listing\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif info.Size > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = info.Size\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\teg.Wait() //nolint:errcheck\n}\n\n// ListObjectsV2 handles S3 ListObjectsV2.\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListObjectsV2(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tprefix := r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n\tdelimiter := r.URL.Query().Get(\"delimiter\")\n\tcontToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"continuation-token\")\n\tmaxKeys := 1000\n\tif mk := r.URL.Query().Get(\"max-keys\"); mk != \"\" {\n\t\tif v, err := strconv.Atoi(mk); err == nil && v > 0 {\n\t\t\tmaxKeys = v\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply the configured prefix to the prefix parameter for backend operations.\n\t// When ARMOR_PREFIX is set, the backend stores all keys with the prefix prepended,\n\t// but clients don't know about it. We need to prepend the prefix to the client's\n\t// requested prefix so the backend finds the right objects.\n\tbackendPrefix := h.applyPrefix(prefix)\n\n\tvar result *backend.ListResult\n\tif h.listCache != nil {\n\t\tif cached, ok := h.listCache.Get(bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken); ok {\n\t\t\tresult = cached\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif result == nil {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tresult, err = h.backend.List(ctx, bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, contToken, maxKeys)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Resolve plaintext sizes for ARMOR-encrypted objects before caching.\n\t\t// B2 ListObjectsV2 returns encrypted (on-disk) sizes; clients like DuckDB\n\t\t// use these sizes to compute byte-range offsets, so they must reflect the\n\t\t// plaintext length.\n\t\th.enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx, bucket, result)\n\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n\t\t\th.listCache.Set(bucket, prefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken, result)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype Contents struct {\n\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n\t}\n\n\t// S3 spec: each CommonPrefixes entry is a separate wrapper element with one\n\t// Prefix child. Using []string with \"Outer>Inner\" produces an empty\n\t// <CommonPrefixes/> when the slice is nil, which the AWS SDK v1 parses as a\n\t// slice with one nil element — causing nil-pointer crashes in clients like\n\t// litestream. Using a dedicated struct + omitempty avoids this.\n\ttype CommonPrefix struct {\n\t\tPrefix string `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListBucketResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName               xml.Name       `xml:\"ListBucketResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns                 string         `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tName                  string         `xml:\"Name\"`\n\t\tPrefix                string         `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n\t\tDelimiter             string         `xml:\"Delimiter,omitempty\"`\n\t\tMaxKeys               int            `xml:\"MaxKeys\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated           bool           `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tNextContinuationToken string         `xml:\"NextContinuationToken,omitempty\"`\n\t\tContents              []Contents\n\t\tCommonPrefixes        []CommonPrefix `xml:\"CommonPrefixes,omitempty\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListBucketResult{\n\t\tXmlns:                 \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tName:                  bucket,\n\t\tPrefix:                prefix,\n\t\tDelimiter:             delimiter,\n\t\tMaxKeys:               maxKeys,\n\t\tIsTruncated:           result.IsTruncated,\n\t\tNextContinuationToken: result.NextToken,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, obj := range result.Objects {\n\t\t// Strip the configured prefix from object keys before returning to client.\n\t\t// Clients don't know about the prefix, so we need to remove it from the keys.\n\t\tstrippedKey := h.stripPrefix(obj.Key)\n\t\tresp.Contents = append(resp.Contents, Contents{\n\t\t\tKey:          strippedKey,\n\t\t\tLastModified: obj.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, obj.ETag),\n\t\t\tSize:         obj.Size,\n\t\t\tStorageClass: \"STANDARD\",\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, p := range result.CommonPrefixes {\n\t\t// Strip the configured prefix from common prefixes before returning to client.\n\t\t// Common prefixes are used for directory-like listings with delimiters.\n\t\tstrippedPrefix := h.stripPrefixFromCommonPrefix(p)\n\t\tresp.CommonPrefixes = append(resp.CommonPrefixes, CommonPrefix{Prefix: strippedPrefix})\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// Stub implementations for other operations\n\n// HeadBucket handles S3 HeadBucket.\nfunc (h *Handlers) HeadBucket(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := h.backend.HeadBucket(ctx, bucket); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchBucket\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Bucket not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// GetBucketLocation handles S3 GetBucketLocation (GET ?location).\n// Returns a static LocationConstraint so MinIO/Forgejo clients can determine the region\n// without requiring region-aware routing.\nfunc (h *Handlers) GetBucketLocation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := h.backend.HeadBucket(ctx, bucket); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchBucket\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Bucket not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttype locationConstraintResponse struct {\n\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"LocationConstraint\"`\n\t\tXMLNS    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tLocation string   `xml:\",chardata\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := locationConstraintResponse{\n\t\tXMLNS:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tLocation: \"\",\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tif _, err := fmt.Fprint(w, xml.Header); err != nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t_ = xml.NewEncoder(w).Encode(resp)\n}\n\n// GetBucketVersioning handles S3 GetBucketVersioning (GET ?versioning).\n// Returns an empty VersioningConfiguration (versioning is not enabled on ARMOR buckets).\nfunc (h *Handlers) GetBucketVersioning(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := h.backend.HeadBucket(ctx, bucket); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchBucket\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Bucket not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttype versioningConfigurationResponse struct {\n\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"VersioningConfiguration\"`\n\t\tXMLNS   string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := versioningConfigurationResponse{\n\t\tXMLNS: \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tif _, err := fmt.Fprint(w, xml.Header); err != nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t_ = xml.NewEncoder(w).Encode(resp)\n}\n\n// CreateBucket handles S3 CreateBucket.\nfunc (h *Handlers) CreateBucket(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := h.backend.CreateBucket(ctx, bucket); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create bucket: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Location\", \"/\"+bucket)\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// DeleteBucket handles S3 DeleteBucket.\nfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteBucket(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := h.backend.DeleteBucket(ctx, bucket); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to delete bucket: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// DeleteObjects handles S3 DeleteObjects (bulk delete).\n// The request body contains XML with a list of objects to delete.\nfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObjects(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Parse the DeleteObjects request XML\n\ttype Object struct {\n\t\tKey string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype DeleteRequest struct {\n\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Delete\"`\n\t\tObjects []Object `xml:\"Object\"`\n\t\tQuiet   bool     `xml:\"Quiet\"`\n\t}\n\n\tvar deleteReq DeleteRequest\n\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &deleteReq); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif len(deleteReq.Objects) == 0 {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", \"No objects specified for deletion\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract keys\n\tkeys := make([]string, len(deleteReq.Objects))\n\tfor i, obj := range deleteReq.Objects {\n\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n\t}\n\n\t// Perform bulk delete\n\tif err := h.backend.DeleteObjects(ctx, bucket, keys); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"DeleteObjects failed: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove from manifest\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Invalidate cache for deleted objects\n\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n\t\th.footerCache.Delete(bucket, key)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build response XML\n\ttype DeletedObject struct {\n\t\tKey string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype DeleteResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName xml.Name        `xml:\"DeleteResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns   string          `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tDeleted []DeletedObject `xml:\"Deleted\"`\n\t\tError   []struct {\n\t\t\tKey     string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\t\tCode    string `xml:\"Code\"`\n\t\t\tMessage string `xml:\"Message\"`\n\t\t} `xml:\"Error\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresult := DeleteResult{\n\t\tXmlns: \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t}\n\n\t// If not quiet mode, include all deleted keys\n\tif !deleteReq.Quiet {\n\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\t\tresult.Deleted = append(result.Deleted, DeletedObject{Key: key})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// ListBuckets handles S3 ListBuckets.\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListBuckets(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tbuckets, err := h.backend.ListBuckets(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list buckets: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype Bucket struct {\n\t\tName         string `xml:\"Name\"`\n\t\tCreationDate string `xml:\"CreationDate\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListAllMyBucketsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"ListAllMyBucketsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns   string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tOwner   struct {\n\t\t\tID          string `xml:\"ID\"`\n\t\t\tDisplayName string `xml:\"DisplayName\"`\n\t\t} `xml:\"Owner\"`\n\t\tBuckets struct {\n\t\t\tBucket []Bucket `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\t} `xml:\"Buckets\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresult := ListAllMyBucketsResult{\n\t\tXmlns: \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t}\n\tresult.Owner.ID = \"armor\"\n\tresult.Owner.DisplayName = \"ARMOR\"\n\n\tfor _, b := range buckets {\n\t\tresult.Buckets.Bucket = append(result.Buckets.Bucket, Bucket{\n\t\t\tName:         b.Name,\n\t\t\tCreationDate: b.CreationDate.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// CreateMultipartUpload handles S3 CreateMultipartUpload with ARMOR encryption.\n// It generates a DEK and IV, wraps the DEK, and stores the state in B2.\nfunc (h *Handlers) CreateMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV for this upload\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get content type\n\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Initiate multipart upload with B2 (no ARMOR metadata yet - that comes on completion)\n\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Save multipart state to B2\n\tstate := &backend.MultipartState{\n\t\tUploadID:       uploadID,\n\t\tBucket:         bucket,\n\t\tKey:            key,\n\t\tIV:             iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:     wrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:      h.config.BlockSize,\n\t\tCreated:        time.Now(),\n\t\tContentType:    contentType,\n\t\tKeyID:          keyID,\n\t\tEncryptedBytes: 0,\n\t\tPartHMACs:      make(map[int]string),\n\t\tPartSizes:      make(map[int]int64),\n\t}\n\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n\t\t// Try to abort the upload on state save failure\n\t\th.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype InitiateMultipartUploadResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"InitiateMultipartUploadResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tUploadID string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresult := InitiateMultipartUploadResult{\n\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n\t\tKey:      key,\n\t\tUploadID: uploadID,\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// UploadPart handles S3 UploadPart with encryption.\n// Each part is encrypted with a CTR counter offset based on cumulative encrypted bytes.\nfunc (h *Handlers) UploadPart(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Parse part number\n\tpartNumberStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\")\n\tif partNumberStr == \"\" {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing partNumber\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tpartNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(partNumberStr, 10, 32)\n\tif err != nil || partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Invalid partNumber\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Load multipart state\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Read plaintext part\n\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n\n\t// VALIDATION: Reject unsupported multipart patterns that would cause silent corruption\n\t// See docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8 and bead bf-59unr3\n\n\t// U7: Reject part retries (same partNumber uploaded more than once)\n\tif _, exists := state.PartSizes[int(partNumber)]; exists {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidPart\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Part %d has already been uploaded. ARMOR does not support part retries due to CTR counter derivation from cumulative bytes. Retry the entire multipart upload from the beginning.\", partNumber), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// U6: Reject out-of-order parts (parts must be uploaded sequentially)\n\t// The expected part number is derived from the count of already-uploaded parts\n\texpectedPartNumber := len(state.PartSizes) + 1\n\tif int(partNumber) != expectedPartNumber {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidPartOrder\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Parts must be uploaded in sequential order. Expected part %d, got part %d. ARMOR does not support out-of-order or concurrent part uploads due to CTR counter derivation from cumulative bytes.\", expectedPartNumber, partNumber), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// U8: Reject non-block-aligned intermediate parts\n\t// All parts except the last must be multiples of BlockSize (65536 bytes for AES)\n\t// We don't know if this is the last part at upload time, so we enforce alignment for all parts.\n\t// Clients can work around this by using a part size that's a multiple of BlockSize.\n\tif plaintextSize > 0 && plaintextSize%int64(state.BlockSize) != 0 {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidPartSize\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Part size %d is not a multiple of the block size (%d bytes). ARMOR's CTR counter derivation requires all parts to be block-aligned. Use a part size that's a multiple of %d (e.g., 5,242,880 for 5MiB, 16,777,216 for 16MiB).\", plaintextSize, state.BlockSize, state.BlockSize), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(state.KeyID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, state.WrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate starting block index for CTR counter\n\t// Each block is blockSize bytes, so counter = encryptedBytes / blockSize\n\tstartBlockIndex := uint32(state.EncryptedBytes / int64(state.BlockSize))\n\n\t// Create encryptor\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, state.IV, state.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt the part with the correct starting counter\n\t// For multipart uploads, each part continues the CTR stream from where\n\t// the previous part left off to maintain cryptographic continuity\n\tencrypted, blockHMACsRaw, err := encryptor.EncryptWithStartingCounter(plaintext, startBlockIndex)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Upload encrypted part to B2\n\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, int32(partNumber), bytes.NewReader(encrypted), int64(len(encrypted)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Split concatenated HMACs into individual HMACs for storage\n\t// The EncryptWithStartingCounter method returns HMACs as a flat byte slice\n\tblockCount := len(blockHMACsRaw) / crypto.HMACSize\n\tblockHMACs := make([][]byte, blockCount)\n\tfor i := 0; i < blockCount; i++ {\n\t\tblockHMACs[i] = blockHMACsRaw[i*crypto.HMACSize : (i+1)*crypto.HMACSize]\n\t}\n\n\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n\n\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\nfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the CompleteMultipartUpload request XML\n\ttype Part struct {\n\t\tPartNumber int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n\t\tETag       string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadReq struct {\n\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n\t\tParts   []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n\t}\n\n\tvar completeReq CompleteMultipartUploadReq\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &completeReq); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif len(completeReq.Parts) == 0 {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"No parts specified\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert to backend.CompletedPart\n\tparts := make([]backend.CompletedPart, len(completeReq.Parts))\n\tfor i, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n\t\tparts[i] = backend.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(p.PartNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:       p.ETag,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate total plaintext size\n\tvar totalPlaintextSize int64\n\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n\t\t\ttotalPlaintextSize += size\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Assemble all block HMACs in order\n\tvar allBlockHMACs [][]byte\n\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n\t\tif hmacsBase64, ok := state.PartHMACs[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n\t\t\thmacs, err := backend.DecodeHMACFromBase64(hmacsBase64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode HMACs for part %d: %v\", p.PartNumber, err), 500)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tallBlockHMACs = append(allBlockHMACs, hmacs...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Complete the multipart upload in B2\n\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to complete multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Store HMAC table as sidecar\n\tif err := manager.SaveHMACTable(ctx, key, allBlockHMACs, state.BlockSize); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256 (we don't have the full plaintext, so use a placeholder)\n\t// In a full implementation, we'd track SHA during upload\n\tplaintextSHA := sha256.Sum256([]byte{})\n\n\t// Build ARMOR metadata and update via CopyObject\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     state.BlockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: totalPlaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   state.ContentType,\n\t\tIV:            state.IV,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    state.WrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n\n\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart state\n\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n\n\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, totalPlaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), state.IV, state.WrappedDEK, state.BlockSize, state.ContentType, etag)\n\t}\n\n\t// Record provenance for the multipart upload\n\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), \"multipart\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUploadResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tLocation string   `xml:\"Location\"`\n\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tETag     string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresult := CompleteMultipartUploadResult{\n\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tLocation: fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s\", bucket, key),\n\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n\t\tKey:      key,\n\t\tETag:     etag,\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// AbortMultipartUpload handles S3 AbortMultipartUpload.\n// It deletes the multipart state and forwards the abort to B2.\nfunc (h *Handlers) AbortMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state to verify it exists\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Forward abort to B2\n\tif err := h.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to abort multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart state\n\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// ListParts handles S3 ListParts operation.\n// It forwards to B2 and adjusts part sizes to plaintext sizes.\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListParts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state to get plaintext sizes\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Forward to B2\n\tresult, err := h.backend.ListParts(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list parts: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response with plaintext sizes\n\ttype Part struct {\n\t\tPartNumber   int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListPartsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName              xml.Name `xml:\"ListPartsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns                string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tBucket               string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKey                  string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tUploadID             string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t\tStorageClass         string   `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n\t\tPartNumberMarker     int      `xml:\"PartNumberMarker\"`\n\t\tNextPartNumberMarker int      `xml:\"NextPartNumberMarker\"`\n\t\tMaxParts             int      `xml:\"MaxParts\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated          bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tParts                []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListPartsResult{\n\t\tXmlns:                \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tBucket:               bucket,\n\t\tKey:                  key,\n\t\tUploadID:             uploadID,\n\t\tStorageClass:         \"STANDARD\",\n\t\tPartNumberMarker:     result.NextPartNumberMarker,\n\t\tNextPartNumberMarker: result.NextPartNumberMarker,\n\t\tIsTruncated:          result.IsTruncated,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range result.Parts {\n\t\t// Use plaintext size from state if available, otherwise use reported size\n\t\tplaintextSize := part.Size\n\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[int(part.PartNumber)]; ok {\n\t\t\tplaintextSize = size\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp.Parts = append(resp.Parts, Part{\n\t\t\tPartNumber:   int(part.PartNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:         part.ETag,\n\t\t\tSize:         plaintextSize,\n\t\t\tLastModified: part.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// ListMultipartUploads handles S3 ListMultipartUploads operation.\n// It forwards directly to B2 (passthrough operation).\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListMultipartUploads(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tresult, err := h.backend.ListMultipartUploads(ctx, bucket)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list multipart uploads: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype Upload struct {\n\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tUploadID     string `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t\tInitiator    string `xml:\"Initiator>ID\"`\n\t\tOwner        string `xml:\"Owner>ID\"`\n\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n\t\tInitiated    string `xml:\"Initiated\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListMultipartUploadsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName            xml.Name `xml:\"ListMultipartUploadsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns              string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tBucket             string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKeyMarker          string   `xml:\"KeyMarker\"`\n\t\tUploadIDMarker     string   `xml:\"UploadIdMarker\"`\n\t\tNextKeyMarker      string   `xml:\"NextKeyMarker\"`\n\t\tNextUploadIDMarker string   `xml:\"NextUploadIdMarker\"`\n\t\tMaxUploads         int      `xml:\"MaxUploads\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated        bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tUploads            []Upload `xml:\"Upload\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListMultipartUploadsResult{\n\t\tXmlns:              \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tBucket:             bucket,\n\t\tKeyMarker:          r.URL.Query().Get(\"key-marker\"),\n\t\tUploadIDMarker:     r.URL.Query().Get(\"upload-id-marker\"),\n\t\tNextKeyMarker:      result.NextKeyMarker,\n\t\tNextUploadIDMarker: result.NextUploadIDMarker,\n\t\tIsTruncated:        result.IsTruncated,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, upload := range result.Uploads {\n\t\tresp.Uploads = append(resp.Uploads, Upload{\n\t\t\tKey:          upload.Key,\n\t\t\tUploadID:     upload.UploadID,\n\t\t\tInitiator:    \"armor\",\n\t\t\tOwner:        \"armor\",\n\t\t\tStorageClass: \"STANDARD\",\n\t\t\tInitiated:    upload.Initiated.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// ListObjectVersions handles S3 ListObjectVersions operation.\n// It lists all versions of objects in a bucket, For ARMOR-encrypted objects,\n// it retrieves per-version metadata to provide plaintext sizes.\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListObjectVersions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tprefix := r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n\tdelimiter := r.URL.Query().Get(\"delimiter\")\n\tkeyMarker := r.URL.Query().Get(\"key-marker\")\n\tversionIDMarker := r.URL.Query().Get(\"version-id-marker\")\n\tmaxKeys := 1000\n\tif mk := r.URL.Query().Get(\"max-keys\"); mk != \"\" {\n\t\tif v, err := strconv.Atoi(mk); err == nil && v > 0 {\n\t\t\tmaxKeys = v\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult, err := h.backend.ListObjectVersions(ctx, bucket, prefix, delimiter, keyMarker, versionIDMarker, maxKeys)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list object versions: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype Version struct {\n\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tVersionID    string `xml:\"VersionId\"`\n\t\tIsLatest     bool   `xml:\"IsLatest\"`\n\t\tIsDeleteMarker bool `xml:\"IsDeleteMarker,omitempty\"`\n\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag,omitempty\"`\n\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size,omitempty\"`\n\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass,omitempty\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListVersionsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName               xml.Name `xml:\"ListVersionsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns                 string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tName                  string   `xml:\"Name\"`\n\t\tPrefix                string   `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n\t\tDelimiter             string   `xml:\"Delimiter,omitempty\"`\n\t\tMaxKeys               int      `xml:\"MaxKeys\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated           bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tKeyMarker             string   `xml:\"KeyMarker\"`\n\t\tVersionIDMarker       string   `xml:\"VersionIdMarker\"`\n\t\tNextKeyMarker         string   `xml:\"NextKeyMarker\"`\n\t\tNextVersionIDMarker   string   `xml:\"NextVersionIdMarker\"`\n\t\tVersions              []Version `xml:\"Version\"`\n\t\tCommonPrefixes       []string `xml:\"CommonPrefixes>Prefix\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListVersionsResult{\n\t\tXmlns:               \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tName:                bucket,\n\t\tPrefix:              prefix,\n\t\tDelimiter:           delimiter,\n\t\tMaxKeys:             maxKeys,\n\t\tIsTruncated:         result.IsTruncated,\n\t\tKeyMarker:           keyMarker,\n\t\tVersionIDMarker:     versionIDMarker,\n\t\tNextKeyMarker:       result.NextKeyMarker,\n\t\tNextVersionIDMarker: result.NextVersionIDMarker,\n\t}\n\n\t// Process versions and retrieve per-version metadata for ARMOR objects\n\tfor _, version := range result.Versions {\n\t\tv := Version{\n\t\t\tKey:            version.Key,\n\t\t\tVersionID:      version.VersionID,\n\t\t\tIsLatest:       version.IsLatest,\n\t\t\tIsDeleteMarker: version.IsDeleteMarker,\n\t\t\tLastModified:   version.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !version.IsDeleteMarker {\n\t\t\t// For the latest version, try the manifest index first to avoid a\n\t\t\t// per-version HeadObject B2 API call. The manifest tracks current\n\t\t\t// state only, so this optimisation applies only to IsLatest.\n\t\t\tmanifestHit := false\n\t\t\tif h.manifest != nil && version.IsLatest {\n\t\t\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, version.Key); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tv.Size = entry.PlaintextSize\n\t\t\t\t\tv.ETag = fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag)\n\t\t\t\t\tv.StorageClass = \"STANDARD\"\n\t\t\t\t\tmanifestHit = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !manifestHit {\n\t\t\t\t// Fall back to a per-version HeadObject call (non-latest versions\n\t\t\t\t// or manifest miss).\n\t\t\t\tif info, err := h.backend.HeadVersion(ctx, bucket, version.Key, version.VersionID); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tv.Size = am.PlaintextSize\n\t\t\t\t\t\tv.ETag = fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, am.ETag)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tv.StorageClass = \"STANDARD\"\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tv.Size = version.Size\n\t\t\t\t\t\tv.ETag = fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, version.ETag)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tv.StorageClass = \"STANDARD\"\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tv.Size = version.Size\n\t\t\t\t\tv.ETag = fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, version.ETag)\n\t\t\t\t\tv.StorageClass = \"STANDARD\"\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp.Versions = append(resp.Versions, v)\n\t}\n\n\t// Process common prefixes\n\tresp.CommonPrefixes = append(resp.CommonPrefixes, result.CommonPrefixes...)\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration handles GET ?lifecycle on a bucket.\n// This is a passthrough operation - lifecycle configuration is not encrypted.\nfunc (h *Handlers) GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tconfig, err := h.backend.GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx, bucket)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get lifecycle configuration: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" := r.Context()\n\n\t// Read the object lock configuration XML\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n70\t\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n71\t\tif err != nil {\n72\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n73\t\t}\n74\t\tr.rmu.Lock()\n75\t\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n76\t\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n77\t\tif !ok {\n78\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n79\t\t}\n80\t\tparts[partNumber] = data\n81\t\tr.partKeys = append(r.partKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n82\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-part-%d\", partNumber), nil\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\tfunc (r handlers_test\n\n// Tests for the multipart upload HTTP surface, added per ADR-002 and\n// docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md (U4, R4, R7).\n//\n// The 2026-06 corruption bug lived in ROUTING: a standard client's\n// `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` fell through to plain PutObject, storing each\n// part as the whole object. Backend-level tests and the 1KB canary were\n// structurally blind to it. These tests exercise the full HTTP handler path\n// with a recording backend so a routing regression fails loudly.\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/rand\"\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/keymanager\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server/handlers\"\n)\n\n// recordingBackend wraps mockBackend, records which backend methods are\n// invoked per object key, and implements a real in-memory multipart\n// store: parts are kept per uploadID and concatenated (in the part order\n// given to CompleteMultipartUpload) into the object map on completion —\n// mirroring what B2 does on b2_finish_large_file.\ntype recordingBackend struct {\n\t*mockBackend\n\n\trmu      sync.Mutex\n\tputKeys  []string // keys passed to Put (includes state/sidecar writes)\n\tpartKeys []string // keys passed to UploadPart\n\tuploads  map[string]map[int32][]byte\n\tnextID   int\n}\n\nfunc newRecordingBackend() *recordingBackend {\n\treturn &recordingBackend{\n\t\tmockBackend: newMockBackend(),\n\t\tuploads:     make(map[string]map[int32][]byte),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) Put(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, body io.Reader, size int64, meta map[string]string) error {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tr.putKeys = append(r.putKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\treturn r.mockBackend.Put(ctx, bucket, key, body, size, meta)\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tr.nextID++\n\tid := fmt.Sprintf(\"upload-%d\", r.nextID)\n\tr.uploads[id] = make(map[int32][]byte)\n\treturn id, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tparts[partNumber] = data\n\tr.partKeys = append(r.partKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-part-%d\", partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, completed []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tvar assembled []byte\n\tfor _, p := range completed {\n\t\tdata, ok := parts[p.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"missing part %d\", p.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tassembled = append(assembled, data...)\n\t}\n\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\n\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Store assembled object exactly as B2 would, preserving no metadata —\n\t// the handler applies ARMOR metadata afterwards via Copy.\n\tr.mockBackend.mu.Lock()\n\tr.mockBackend.objects[bucket+\"/\"+key] = assembled\n\tif _, ok := r.mockBackend.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key]; !ok {\n\t\tr.mockBackend.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key] = map[string]string{}\n\t}\n\tr.mockBackend.mu.Unlock()\n\treturn \"etag-assembled\", nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// objectPutCount returns how many times Put was called with exactly this key.\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) objectPutCount(bucket, key string) int {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tn := 0\n\tfor _, k := range r.putKeys {\n\t\tif k == bucket+\"/\"+key {\n\t\t\tn++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn n\n}\n\nfunc recordingTestSetup(t *testing.T) (*config.Config, *recordingBackend, *handlers.Handlers) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(mek); err != nil Until the design is fixed (per-part counters derived from\n// partNumber × known part size, or a hard 400 on unsupported patterns),\n// this test stays skipped as an executable TODO. Unskipping it without a\n// design fix will (correctly) fail.\nfunc TestMultipartSuspectPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Skip(\"KNOWN DESIGN GAP bf-59unr3 (matrix U6/U7/U8): out-of-order, retried, and non-aligned parts corrupt silently; clients must be pinned to sequential aligned no-retry behavior until fixed\")\n}","new_string":"// TestMultipartSuspectPatterns documents and enforces rejection of the three\n// multipart upload patterns that real SDKs use by default but ARMOR's\n// per-part CTR-offset scheme does not support (docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8):\n//\n//\tU6 out-of-order / parallel part upload  (boto3 uploads parts concurrently)\n//\tU7 part retry after network failure     (every SDK retries parts)\n//\tU8 non-block-aligned intermediate parts (any part size % 65536 != 0)\n//\n// UploadPart derives each part's CTR counter from arrival-order cumulative\n// EncryptedBytes (handlers.go:2085), so all three would corrupt silently.\n// The fix implemented in bf-59unr3 rejects these patterns with explicit 400\n// errors rather than allowing silent corruption.\nfunc TestMultipartSuspectPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"U6_out_of_order_parts\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"out-of-order.dat\"\n\t\tconst block = 65536\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Upload part 1 first (succeeds)\n\t\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\n\t\tfor i := range part1 {\n\t\t\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload part 3 without uploading part 2 first (should fail)\n\t\tpart3 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\n\t\tfor i := range part3 {\n\t\t\tpart3[i] = 0xCC\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=3&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part3))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for out-of-order part, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"Parts must be uploaded in sequential order\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message about sequential order, got: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"U6_parallel_parts_simulation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"parallel-sim.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload part 2 before part 1 (simulates parallel upload where part 2 arrives first)\n\t\tpart2 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\n\t\tfor i := range part2 {\n\t\t\tpart2[i] = 0xBB\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=2&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part2))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part 2 before part 1, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"Expected part 1, got part 2\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message about expecting part 1, got: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"U7_part_retry\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"retry-test.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Upload part 1 successfully\n\t\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\n\t\tfor i := range part1 {\n\t\t\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload part 1 again (simulating retry after network failure)\n\t\tpart1Retry := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\n\t\tfor i := range part1Retry {\n\t\t\tpart1Retry[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part1Retry))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part retry, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"has already been uploaded\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message about already uploaded, got: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"U8_non_block_aligned_part\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"unaligned-test.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload a part with non-block-aligned size (10,000,000 bytes)\n\t\t// 10,000,000 % 65536 = 16976 (not aligned)\n\t\tpart := make([]byte, 10_000_000)\n\t\tfor i := range part {\n\t\t\tpart[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for non-block-aligned part, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"not a multiple of the block size\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message about block alignment, got: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"U8_zero_byte_part_allowed\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Edge case: zero-byte parts should be allowed (can happen with empty files)\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"zero-byte.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Zero-byte part should not trigger the alignment check\n\t\tpart := make([]byte, 0)\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Zero-byte parts should fail for a different reason (not alignment)\n\t\t// The actual error might be about empty body or size, but not the alignment check\n\t\tif w.Code == http.StatusBadRequest && bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"not a multiple of the block size\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zero-byte part should not trigger block alignment error: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"805a8815-63d6-4b7a-adda-ceb49c557c44","uuid":"47a6544b-f2d0-47d8-91d4-f28fcbfde1c8"}

*testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"out-of-order.dat\"\n\t\tconst block = 65536\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Upload part 1 first (succeeds)\n\t\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\n\t\tfor i := range part1 {\n\t\t\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload part 3 without uploading part 2 first (should fail)\n\t\tpart3 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\n\t\tfor i := range part3 {\n\t\t\tpart3[i] = 0xCC\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for out-of-order part, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"Parts must be uploaded in sequential order\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message about sequential order, got: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"U6_parallel_parts_simulation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"parallel-sim.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload part 2 before part 1 (simulates parallel upload where part 2 arrives first)\n\t\tpart2 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\n\t\tfor i := range part2 {\n\t\t\tpart2[i] = 0xBB\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=2&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part2))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part 2 before part 1, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"Expected part 1, got part 2\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message about expecting part 1, got: %s\", w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"U7_part_retry\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\t\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"retry-test.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Upload part 1 successfully\n\t\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\n\t\tfor make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\n\t\tfor i := range part1Retry {\n\t\t\tpart1Retry[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part1Retry))\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part retry, got %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\"has already been uploaded\")) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message \"test-bucket\", \"unaligned-test.dat\"\n\n\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\n\t\t// Try to upload a part with non-block-aligned size (10,000,000 bytes)\n\t\t// 10,000,000 % 65536 = 16976 (not aligned)\n\t\tpart := make([]byte, 10_000_000)\n\t\tfor i := range part {\n\t\t\tpart[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t}\n\t\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key,
